data.title: Expert Criticizes Federal Reserve's Basel III Revisions for Bitcoin
data.description: Pierre Rochard has expressed concerns over the Federal Reserve's proposed Basel III revisions, highlighting the lack of clarity in Bitcoin capital rules. According to NS3.AI, the current Basel framework assigns Bitcoin a 1250% risk weight, which Rochard believes could pose legal risks for large banks involved in Bitcoin services. He urged the Federal Reserve, FDIC, and OCC to avoid finalizing the rules without a clear rationale and framework.

data.title: The Israeli military claims to have struck approximately 40 Iranian weapons production and research facilities.
data.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) issued a statement on the 30th, saying that it had launched multiple airstrikes against Tehran, the Iranian capital, over the past two days, targeting approximately 40 weapons production and research facilities. The statement said that the Israeli Air Force used more than 80 munitions on the 29th, primarily targeting Iranian weapons production facilities, including a facility assembling long-range surface-to-air missiles, a facility producing components for anti-tank missiles and small anti-aircraft missiles, and a facility producing and developing ballistic missile engines. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman: As long as Iran remains a member state of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, it will abide by the treaty.
data.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Bagaei stated that Iran will abide by the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons as long as it remains a member state, but its effectiveness is questionable due to attacks on Iran and the lack of respect for its rights. (Jinshi)

data.title: Israeli Airstrikes Target Iranian Weapon Facilities in Tehran
data.description: The Israeli Defense Forces announced on the 30th that they conducted multiple airstrikes on Tehran, Iran's capital, over the past two days. According to Odaily, these strikes targeted approximately 40 weapon production and research facilities. On the 29th, the Israeli Air Force deployed over 80 munitions, focusing on Iranian weapon production sites. These included a facility for assembling long-range surface-to-air missiles, a site for producing components for anti-tank and small air defense missiles, and a facility for the production and development of ballistic missile engines.

data.title: India's Deputy Finance Minister: Trade Channels May Impact GDP
data.description: India's Deputy Finance Minister has expressed concerns that the country's GDP could be affected through trade channels. According to Jin10, the official highlighted the likelihood of import-driven inflation impacting both consumption and investment in India. The statement underscores the potential economic challenges facing the nation as it navigates global trade dynamics.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks generally rose in pre-market trading in the US, with BMNR up 3.81%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, crypto stocks generally rose in pre-market trading in the US, with COIN up 2.4%, MSTR up 1.88%, SBET up 3.31%, and BMNR up 3.81%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Binance Alpha has listed R2, currently trading at 0.047 USDT.
data.description: According to the official interface, Binance Alpha has listed R2 (R2 Procotol), currently trading at 0.047 USDT.

data.title: UK Economic Growth Forecast for 2026 Lowered Due to Middle East Conflict
data.description: Economists at Pantheon Macroeconomics have revised the UK's economic growth forecast for 2026 from 0.9% to 0.5%, citing the ongoing Middle East conflict as a significant factor. According to Jin10, the economists noted that there are currently no signs of the conflict ending soon, which adds uncertainty to its overall impact on global growth and inflation. They suggest that the risks are skewed towards lower growth and higher inflation for the UK. Additionally, the institution has raised its UK inflation forecast for 2026 from 2.6% to 3.3%.

data.title: Gate has launched BASED (Based) perpetual contract trading.
data.description: According to official sources, Gate has now launched BASED (Based) perpetual contract trading (USDT settlement), supporting leverage from 1 to 20 times.

data.title: Expert: Middle East Tensions Boost Oil Prices and Strengthen Dollar
data.description: Chris Turner from ING has indicated that concerns over a potential escalation in the Middle East are driving up oil prices and negatively impacting risk sentiment. According to NS3.AI, this situation is bolstering the U.S. dollar. Turner noted that the dollar is unlikely to lose its recent gains unless there is a definitive de-escalation signal from Iran.

data.title: ECB Faces Inflation Concerns Amid Rising Euro-Area Consumer Expectations
data.description: Inflation expectations among euro-area consumers surged in March, raising concerns for the European Central Bank (ECB) as it evaluates the potential for a renewed price spike. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the ongoing conflict in Iran on economic stability. The ECB is closely monitoring these developments as it considers its monetary policy options to address inflationary pressures.

data.title: ING: The US dollar strengthens again due to concerns about escalating war.
data.description: The dollar strengthened again as concerns about escalating conflict in the Middle East pushed up oil prices and dampened risk sentiment. Previously, a stronger yen had briefly caused the dollar to retreat slightly after Japanese officials issued stronger warnings about possible intervention to support the yen. However, the dollar's decline was limited and short-lived, supported by its safe-haven appeal and the US's status as a net oil exporter. Chris Turner of ING stated in a report, "Unless Iran sends clear signals of de-escalation, it's difficult to see the dollar giving back its gains for the month in the short term." (Jinshi)

data.title: Southbound Funds Record Net Sell-off of 2.467 Billion Yuan in Hong Kong Stocks
data.description: Southbound funds experienced a net sell-off totaling 2.467 billion yuan today. According to Jin10, within the Hong Kong Stock Connect (Shanghai), the Tracker Fund of Hong Kong and Alibaba-W saw net sell-offs of 1.228 billion HKD and 780 million HKD, respectively. Tencent Holdings led in net buying, with an amount of 437 million HKD. In the Hong Kong Stock Connect (Shenzhen), the Tracker Fund of Hong Kong and Southern Hang Seng Technology recorded net sell-offs of 2.618 billion HKD and 1.338 billion HKD, respectively, while Tencent Holdings again topped net buying with 172 million HKD.

data.title: Portugal Anticipates Bids from Major Airlines for TAP Stake
data.description: Portugal is optimistic that Europe's three largest airline groups will submit bids for a stake in TAP, the national airline, as the deadline approaches this week. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's expectation that these major players will show interest in acquiring a portion of TAP. The move is part of Portugal's broader strategy to strengthen its aviation sector and ensure TAP's competitive position in the market. The anticipated bids are seen as a crucial step in the airline's future development and sustainability.

data.title: Binance Futures to Introduce USDⓈ-Margined BASEDUSDT Perpetual Contract
data.description: Binance Futures is set to launch a new USDⓈ-margined BASEDUSDT perpetual contract on March 30, 2026. According to BWEnews, this announcement marks a significant addition to the platform's offerings. The BASEDUSDT contract will allow traders to engage in perpetual trading with USDⓈ as the margin currency. The current market capitalization of BASED is approximately $34 million, although this figure may be subject to change.

data.title: STOCKS | S&P 500 Correction Approaches Final Stage Amid Ongoing Iran Conflict
data.description: Morgan Stanley strategists have indicated that the S&P 500 correction is nearing its final stage, despite the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes continue to pose a threat to stock markets. The strategists emphasize the potential impact of monetary policy on market stability, suggesting that investors remain cautious as geopolitical tensions persist.

data.title: Iran Strengthens Control Over Strait of Hormuz Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: After a month of fighting, Iran has reportedly achieved a significant strategic advantage by tightening its control over traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the geopolitical implications of this development. The Strait of Hormuz is a crucial maritime passage for global oil shipments, and Iran's increased influence in the region could impact international trade and energy markets. This strategic move comes amid ongoing tensions and military engagements in the area, raising concerns among global powers about the stability and security of this vital shipping lane.

data.title: Iran's First Vice President: If you want to negotiate on the Strait of Hormuz issue, you must promise not to invade Iran.
data.description: According to the Wall Street Journal, Iran's First Vice President Mohammad Reza Arif stated that if Iran's "aggressors" want to negotiate on the Strait of Hormuz issue, they must promise not to invade Iran and recognize Iran's international rights.

data.title: Jio Financial Services Shares Decline Amid Insurance Expansion Plans
data.description: Jio Financial Services shares have decreased to Rs. 227, down from Rs. 333.9 in early August 2025. According to NS3.AI, this decline comes as the company announces its intention to enter the life and general insurance sectors. CEO and MD Hitesh Sethia stated that the rollout is anticipated in 2026, pending regulatory approvals.
